summary: No process.on('unhandledRejection') or 'uncaughtException' handlers in live source
details: The entire packages/ directory has zero process-level error handlers. Default Node/Bun behavior crashes on any unhandled rejection or uncaught exception. Archived artifacts in docs/ show these handlers previously existed but were removed during a refactor and never reintroduced. Risk: process crash on any unhandled async error with no diagnostic dump.

summary: WebSocket error handlers silently swallow errors with empty callbacks
details: Multiple socket.on('error', () => {}) empty handlers at ws.ts:79,99,111,140 and ws-pool.ts:247. Errors are caught to prevent crashes but silently discarded with no logging. While intentional for protocol-level error handling, adding a debug log would improve observability of network issues.

summary: SSE disconnect handlers log but don't clean up GlobalBus subscriptions
details: The event.ts and global.ts SSE handlers register GlobalBus.on('event', handler) subscriptions that may not be torn down on SSE disconnect. Stream.ensuring only logs — no explicit unsubscribe path. 3 call sites affected: event.ts, global.ts, workspace.ts.

summary: WebSocket pool doesn't close/deregister failed sockets — dead socket reference leak
details: When onConnectionInvalid fires (non-retryable terminal event), the pool increments failure counter and falls back to HTTP, but never closes or removes the failed socket from the pool entry. The dead socket reference leaks. Also, streamFailures is reset to 0 on new entries, so repeatedly failing connections cycle indefinitely.

summary: SSE reconnect backoff mentioned in comment but not implemented
details: Comment says 'Back off reconnect attempts up to 2 minutes' but no exponential backoff implementation exists. The waitEvent utility has a fixed timeout with no multiplier.

summary: fire-and-forget setTimeout without cleanup in acp-next/service.ts
details: setTimeout with no variable storage, no clearTimeout, no Effect cleanup. Timer fires unconditionally even if the connection/session context is stale. This can execute stale callbacks after the session is disposed.

summary: fire-and-forget setTimeout without cleanup in acp/agent.ts
details: Same pattern as acp-next/service.ts: setTimeout with no variable storage, no clearTimeout, no Effect cleanup. Fires unconditionally even if the agent session is disposed.

summary: setInterval with no clearInterval in cli/heap.ts — background leak
details: Module-level setInterval runs every 60s for entire process lifetime. No exported stop() or cleanup mechanism. Mitigated slightly by unref() but still a background timer leak. Only runs if OPENCODE_AUTO_HEAP_SNAPSHOT flag is set.
4 bare wrappers around fs/promises.readFile with no try/catch, no .catch(), no Effect.catch. Used across the codebase. Any ENOENT/EACCES/EISDIR error propagates as an unhandled rejection.

summary: config.ts:468 fsNode.writeFile writes config.json without error handling
details: Adjacent unlink(legacy) call has .catch() at L469/471 but the writeFile call itself has no error handling. Config corruption on disk full/permissions would crash.

summary: 12 fs.rm calls in lsp/server.ts with force:true only handle ENOENT — EACCES still crashes
details: 12 fs.rm({force: true}) calls across LSP server installers for cleanup. force:true suppresses ENOENT but not EACCES, EBUSY, ENOSPC. Any permissions or device-busy error causes unhandled rejection.

summary: util/defer.ts:4 void fn() in dispose can cause unhandled rejection if fn returns rejecting Promise
details: Dispose symbol calls void fn() where fn may return Promise<void>. If the promise rejects (e.g., cleanup operation fails), the rejection is unhandled. Should use try/catch or .catch() inside the dispose method.

summary: 10 Database.use() call sites in regular functions with no Effect/try-catch — crash risk
details: 10 call sites where Database.use() is called from regular (non-Effect) functions. A DB connection failure (database locked, disconnected, corrupt) throws uncaught exceptions. Files: project/project.ts (3), session/session.ts (3), session/message-v2.ts (2), server/fence.ts, projectors.ts, control-plane/workspace.ts.

summary: 27 Database.use() inside Effect.fn but not wrapped in Effect.sync — silent defect on DB failure
details: Database.use() inside Effect.gen/Effect.fn generators but not wrapped in Effect.sync(). Synchronous DB access throws become untyped defects instead of typed errors. Spread across session/message-v2.ts, v2/session.ts, session/session.ts, session/prompt.ts, session/projectors*.ts, sync/index.ts, claims.ts.

summary: 6 db.all() queries in event-queries.ts return raw Promises without .catch() or Effect.tryPromise
details: All 6 query functions (queryFailureHotspots, querySlowTools, queryFileContention, queryPhaseGateDenials, queryErrorSummaries, querySessionEvents) return raw db.all() Promises with no error handling. DB failures cause unhandled promise rejections.
